macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Stock Markets Surge as Strait of Hormuz Reopens, Oil Prices Plummet
items.summary: Global stock markets rallied with the S&P 500 up 1.3% to a new record on Friday morning following news of the Strait of Hormuz reopening, easing fears of prolonged energy supply disruptions. Oil prices dropped 14% to $81 per barrel, reducing inflationary pressures and boosting Fed rate cut odds to 50% by year-end.[1]

items.title: Fed Governor Waller Cautious on Rate Cuts Amid Iran Conflict Risks
items.summary: In a speech, Fed Governor Waller noted March headline PCE inflation around 3.5% and core at 3.2% due to energy price surges from the Iran conflict, warning markets undervalue risks of Strait of Hormuz remaining closed. He favors delaying rate cuts until the outlook stabilizes, focusing on labor market support later.[3]

items.title: Trump Signals Iran Agreement, Easing Global Economic Fears
items.summary: President Trump announced Iran agreed to suspend its nuclear program and stop targeting vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, diminishing risks of extended disruptions to oil, LNG, and commodities. This has lowered recession odds, with bond yields falling and markets pricing in Fed easing.[1][2]

items.title: Upcoming Earnings and Data to Watch Amid Iran Developments
items.summary: Key corporate earnings from Tesla, Intel, UnitedHealth, Amex, P&G, and others are due next week, alongside US retail sales expected up 1.3%, PMIs, and Fed nominee Kevin Warsh's testimony on balance sheet policy. Iran-US agreement talks continue to dominate energy and economic outlooks.[2]

items.title: Global Energy Disruptions Persist Despite De-escalation Signals
items.summary: Even with Strait reopening hopes, Europe and Asia face skyrocketing natural gas prices, fertilizer shortages, and rationing from prior Hormuz closure, with economists warning of lagged inflationary effects and economic slowdown risks.[4]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits Two-Month High of $77,400 After Iran Reopens Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Bitcoin surged 5% to $77,400, its highest in two months, following Iran's announcement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, boosting crypto stocks like Strategy, Robinhood, and Coinbase. Ethereum climbed to $2,440, up 3%, while altcoins like XRP rose 2% to $1.49.

items.title: Kraken Parent Payward Acquires Bitnomial for Up to $550 Million
items.summary: Payward, Kraken's parent, agreed to buy derivatives exchange Bitnomial for up to $550 million in a cash-and-stock deal valuing Payward at $20 billion, gaining access to advanced crypto settlement and 24/7 trading infrastructure.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Reach $745-759 Million in 24 Hours
items.summary: Crypto futures saw massive liquidations totaling $745 million to $759 million across exchanges, with short positions hit hardest at $594 million while longs lost $165 million.

items.title: XRP ETF Funds Surpass $1 Billion as Price Jumps Past $1.40
items.summary: XRP ETF funds exceeded $1 billion, driving a 3% price surge above $1.40 and outpacing Bitcoin's weekly gains, amid integration into Solana’s DeFi and efforts to attract $100 billion in DeFi capital.

items.title: Polygon Launches sPOL Liquid Staking Token Unlocking $330 Million
items.summary: Polygon introduced sPOL, a liquid staking token that unlocks approximately $330 million in previously staked capital, reshaping liquidity dynamics in its ecosystem.

items.title: Circle Faces Class Action Lawsuit Over $232 Million USDC Theft
items.summary: Stablecoin issuer Circle is sued by Drift Protocol investors for allegedly failing to freeze $232 million in stolen USDC during an April 1 exploit.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Introduces GPT-Rosalind for Life Science Research
items.summary: OpenAI released GPT-Rosalind, a new reasoning model designed specifically for research across biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine.

items.title: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B Model Released
items.summary: Alibaba's Qwen team released the Qwen3.6-35B-A3B model, continuing the expansion of open-source language model options.

items.title: Microsoft Releases MAI-Image-2-Efficient
items.summary: Microsoft announced MAI-Image-2-Efficient, a new AI model focused on efficient image processing capabilities.

items.title: Boston Dynamics Integrates Spot with Gemini Robotics
items.summary: Boston Dynamics announced integration of its Spot robot with Gemini robotics capabilities for enhanced autonomous task execution.

items.title: AI-Generated Misinformation Spreads in Iran Conflict
items.summary: Forensic experts warn that AI-generated videos depicting the Iran war are proliferating, with misinformation being generated by both sides of the conflict.

items.title: AI Agents Disrupting Traditional Corporate Job Structures
items.summary: AI agents are increasingly being deployed as digital workforces within enterprises, disrupting traditional corporate hierarchies and job roles.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Clair Obscur and Dispatch Lead 2026 BAFTA Games Awards Winners
items.summary: Clair Obscur and Dispatch topped the 2026 BAFTA Games Awards, with Expedition 33 winning Best Game. Ghost of Yotei star Erika Ishii expressed interest in reprising her role.

items.title: Metro 2039 Confirmed for Release This Year
items.summary: 4A Games announced Metro 2039 is coming in 2026, noting the game was fundamentally changed due to Russia's illegal war.

items.title: SNK Releasing Modern NeoGeo AES Console
items.summary: SNK unveiled a modern NeoGeo AES console with unrivaled authenticity and £70 game cartridges, fully compatible with original games.

items.title: Starfield Rated for Nintendo Switch 2
items.summary: Bethesda's Starfield has received a rating for Nintendo Switch 2, strongly indicating an upcoming port.

items.title: Hades 2 Launches on Consoles with Major Update
items.summary: Hades 2 released on PS5 and Xbox alongside a new update that simplifies romancing a popular character, available for all versions.

items.title: Sony State of Play Reveals Multiple Major Titles
items.summary: Sony's June 2025 State of Play showcased Bloodstained: The Scarlet Engagement, 007 First Light (2026 release), Digimon Story: Time Stranger release date, Pragmata release date, and Final Fantasy Tactics: The Ivalice Chronicles.

items.title: Nioh 3 Announced and Playable Immediately
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announced Nioh 3 at Sony’s State of Play, allowing soulslike fans to play it right away.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass June 2025 Wave 1 Lineup Revealed
items.summary: Microsoft confirmed the full list of games joining Xbox Game Pass in the first wave of June 2025 ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase.

items.title: No Man's Sky Overhauls Settlement Mechanics
items.summary: The latest No Man's Sky update completely transforms settlement mechanics and includes a free update for the Switch 2 version.

items.title: PlayStation Plus Extra April Games Include Horizon Remastered
items.summary: PlayStation Plus Extra lineup for April reportedly features Horizon Remastered and more titles, claimable starting April 21.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Kevin Weil Departs OpenAI; Prism Science App Shuttered
items.summary: Kevin Weil, OpenAI's VP for Science and former Instagram VP, is leaving the company. The Prism web application for scientists, launched in January, will be folded into Codex.

items.title: Recursive Superintelligence Raises $500M+ at $4B Valuation
items.summary: Recursive Superintelligence, a four-month-old startup founded by ex-DeepMind and OpenAI engineers, has secured over $500 million in funding with backing from Google's venture arm and Nvidia.

items.title: Hesai Announces EXT Lidar with Integrated Color Detection
items.summary: Chinese lidar maker Hesai, a primary supplier for Nvidia's ADAS systems, has unveiled the EXT lidar, claiming it as the industry's first to integrate spatial and color detection capabilities.

items.title: Mac Mini and Mac Studio Face Extended Wait Times Due to AI Demand
items.summary: Some Mac Mini and Mac Studio models are unavailable or facing up to 12-week wait times in the US, with analysts attributing strong demand to AI agent power users.

items.title: Meta Raises Quest 3 and 3S Prices Amid Memory Crisis
items.summary: Meta is increasing prices for the Quest 3 and Quest 3S virtual reality headsets due to rising memory costs.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold Completely Sold Out
items.summary: Samsung's Galaxy Z TriFold foldable phone, marketed as a limited run, has sold out completely.

items.title: India Drops Plan to Force Apple to Preinstall State App
items.summary: India has abandoned its plan to require Apple to preinstall a state-owned application on iPhones.

items.title: Intel Launches Core Series 3 Processors for Budget Laptops
items.summary: Intel has introduced its Core Series 3 processors designed to offer more affordable options for laptop manufacturers.

items.title: Casely Recalls 400,000+ Wireless Power Pods Over Fire Risk
items.summary: The CPSC has issued an urgent recall for over 400,000 Casely wireless power pods due to fire and burn hazards.

items.title: Senator Warren Raises Concerns Over Nvidia's Slurm Acquisition
items.summary: US Senator Elizabeth Warren has voiced concerns about Nvidia's acquisition of SchedMD, warning it could grant the company significant control over critical software powering US government supercomputers.
